body.title: [dependency] Specialization is graduating debt: record property/incapacity/graduation_proof, and fold back only when the general case PROVES the property
body.description:
Akien 2026-07-08: 'We want domains to become LESS specialized over time — graduating to the general class. The more powerful the reasoning engine, the more flexibility we have. We create diversity only where we need it, and when we can fold the diversity into the general case, we do.'

This is the gate-removal staircase applied to domain code: diversity is scaffolding; the proof corpus earns its removal. It needs teeth or it licenses premature generalization.

WORKED EXAMPLE (today, T-inference-migrate-consumers-cutover): the rule triples WERE a compensation. They hand-curated devstral as the coding floor, guaranteeing 'coding runs on a tool-capable model'. We folded that into the general dimensional case because it looked more principled. Nobody wrote the PROPERTY down, so nobody proved the general case still held it. It did not: a tool-less reasoning model took the coding slot and CONFABULATED completion — claimed it wrote a file it never wrote. An UNEARNED FOLD-BACK.

Every compensation (override, flag, crutch) records: property (what it GUARANTEES), incapacity (the executor weakness assumed), graduation_proof (the runnable test that must pass ON THE GENERAL PATH before deletion). FOLD-BACK RULE: delete only when the general case PROVES the same property — a red/green proof, not a judgment call.

Compensations must be ENUMERABLE (a registry), because a silent override never announces it is still there. Then a scraps job re-runs graduation proofs and reports candidates; fold-back becomes routine, not heroism. Each fold-back is a question that got answered and became structure.

DIRECTION OF INHERITANCE: the base is the GENERAL capability; a subclass may only ADD SCAFFOLDING, never add capability. Something genuinely good in a specialization is a PROMOTION CANDIDATE, not a specialization.

LATENT EVIDENCE: BaseDomain.aci_mode's own comment says 'the generalist base is a strong-tier passthrough -> off; CodingDomain (weak local tier) -> on.' The code already knows the crutch exists because the tier is weak — it never recorded the graduation condition. FALSIFIABLE PREDICTION: as Hex's models improve, DS's specialization should SHRINK measurably.

**Test plan:** red->green: every compensation on DSCodingDomain carries property+incapacity+graduation_proof; a graduation run executes each proof on the general path and reports pass/fail; a passing compensation can be deleted and the suite stays green.

**Decision:** decision:D-domains-general-with-device-owned-specializations-2026-07-08

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

**Note [2026-07-09 06:30]:** DAY-CLOSE AUDIT 2026-07-08 (step 17) — THE GRADUATION APPARATUS IS DEAD CODE, verified by import graph: prereg.py has ZERO importers; eval_slice.py has ZERO importers; corpus_verdict.py is imported only by the orphaned prereg.py. io_corpus.py is the only one still wired (device.py + architect_editor). So the machinery that would decide FOLD-BACK exists, sits in the routing proxy where it does not belong, and nothing calls it. That is the mechanical reason fold-back has never happened automatically — the loop was never closed. This ticket must either wire prereg/corpus_verdict/eval_slice into the graduation run or replace them; do not assume they work because they exist (they have no callers and therefore no evidence).
